name: Quoin Financial Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving South Dakota with full-service retail and commercial banking, mortgages, and specialized agricultural lending.
description_long:
Quoin Financial Bank is a community bank headquartered in South Dakota with physical locations in Miller, Highmore, and Sioux Falls. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing depositors with federal insurance protection up to applicable limits.

Quoin offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking services including multiple checking and savings account options (Kasasa Cash, Advantage, Premier tiers), certificates of deposit, money market accounts, IRAs, HSAs, and personal loans. For mortgage services, they employ licensed mortgage lenders (William Erickson NMLS #1612787 and Mike Bellmore NMLS #2267854) who handle home purchases, refinances, equity loans, and HELOCs. The bank also provides card services through Visa, online and mobile banking with deposit capture, and surcharge-free ATM access.

Quoin distinguishes itself through robust agricultural and commercial lending capabilities, offering term loans, lines of credit, equipment financing, real estate loans, SBA loans, and letters of credit. This positions them as a full-service bank with particular strength in serving farm and ranch operations. They also feature competitive CD rates (3.70%-3.80% APY as of April 2026) and specialized products like Christmas Club savings accounts and Credit Checking Reserve Lines of Credit.

As a regional community bank, Quoin provides traditional banking infrastructure without the scale or digital-first design of major national or neo-banks. The organization is locally managed with published leadership and employment opportunities, suggesting commitment to community engagement. Their service offerings are extensive but require in-person or phone interaction for some products, and rates/terms vary by product and account tier. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking and Kasasa Cash Back Checking accounts
Personal and business savings, money market, and Christmas Club accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with competitive APY rates
Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Personal auto loans, personal loans, and lines of credit
Home mortgages, home equity loans, refinances, and HELOCs
Commercial and agricultural loans (term loans, lines of credit, equipment, real estate, SBA)
Visa check cards and Visa credit cards for personal and business
Online banking, bill pay, mobile banking, and mobile deposit capture
Business merchant card services and cash management solutions
Financial calculators and financial education resources
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with federal backing up to applicable limits
Competitive CD rates up to 3.80% APY with $5,000 minimum deposits
Multiple account tiers (Kasasa Cash free checking, Advantage, Premier) for different needs
Specialized agricultural and commercial lending including farm, equipment, and SBA loans
Mobile banking with mobile deposit capture for remote check deposits
Licensed mortgage lenders on staff for home purchase, refinance, and equity lending
Surcharge-free ATM network access for account holders
cons: Limited geographic footprint (only 3 confirmed locations in South Dakota)
No clear online account opening process visible on website; requires contact/branch visit
CD rates require $5,000 minimum deposit, which excludes smaller savers
Agricultural lending focus may not serve all retail customer segments equally
Website does not display personal loan APRs, terms, or eligibility criteria
best_for: Rural and agricultural business owners needing farm equipment, crop, or livestock financing
South Dakota residents seeking FDIC-insured deposit products and mortgages with local service
Small business owners requiring SBA loans, lines of credit, or merchant services
CD investors looking for competitive rates with minimum $5,000 commitments
diagnosis: Quoin Financial Bank is best for South Dakota residents, agricultural businesses, and rural entrepreneurs who value community banking relationships and specialized farm lending. Primary caveat: the bank's limited geographic footprint and lack of visible online account opening may restrict accessibility for customers outside their service areas or those preferring fully digital banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
